---
applyTo: "**/*.{html,css,js,ts,tsx}" 
---

# Front-End Work Instructions

Follow these conventions when editing front-end files in this workspace.

## General principles
- Keep the UI professional, clean, and presentation-ready.
- Prefer small, targeted changes over large rewrites.
- Preserve the existing visual style unless the user explicitly requests a redesign.
- Maintain responsive behavior for desktop and smaller screens.
- Use semantic HTML and accessible labels whenever possible.

## Styling
- Keep styling consistent with the current palette: blue, slate, white, and neutral tones.
- Favor clear spacing, readable typography, and simple component structure.
- Avoid unnecessary complexity in CSS.
- Reuse existing class patterns where possible.

## Forms and layouts
- Keep form sections logically grouped and easy to scan.
- Preserve field labels, required indicators, and navigation flow.
- Maintain consistency across similar pages and prototypes.

## JavaScript
- Keep scripts lightweight and easy to follow.
- Prefer progressive enhancement over heavy interactivity.
- Avoid introducing frameworks or build tools unless explicitly requested.

## Output expectations
- Summarize what changed and why.
- Mention any assumptions or follow-up suggestions clearly.
